title: Exposed Gmail App Password in Version Control
description: GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD=odsq hraj soqe hyzm is hardcoded in the docker-compose.yml file. This is a live credential that must be rotated immediately.
impact: Any attacker with repository access can compromise the Gmail account and potentially gain access to other linked services.
recommendation: 1. Rotate the Gmail App Password immediately in Google Account settings. 2. Move all credentials to environment variables or Docker secrets. 3. Add this file to .gitignore and remove from git history using git-filter-branch or BFG Repo-Cleaner.

title: Wildcard CORS with Credentials Enabled
description: CORS allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is a security anti-pattern that allows any website to make authenticated cross-origin requests.
impact: Allows malicious websites to make authenticated API requests on behalf of logged-in users, potentially leading to data theft or unauthorized actions.
recommendation: Replace wildcard with explicit allowed origins from environment variable. Never use allow_credentials=True with wildcard origins. Load origins from VIGIL_CORS_ORIGINS env var.

title: JWT Secret Auto-Generation with Race Conditions
description: JWT secret auto-generates if file doesn't exist, with race condition between existence check and write. Permissions set after write creates window where secret is readable.
impact: Race condition could lead to predictable secrets or auth failures. Container recreation causes all tokens to become invalid.
recommendation: Remove auto-generation. Require explicit VIGIL_JWT_SECRET or VIGIL_JWT_SECRET_FILE environment variable. If file used, verify ownership and permissions before reading.

title: Network Mode Host Removes Docker Isolation
description: Using host network mode exposes containers directly to the host network stack, bypassing Docker's network isolation. This allows containers to access all host network interfaces.
impact: Containers can access all host network interfaces, increasing attack surface significantly. Makes port mapping ineffective and prevents use of Docker DNS/service discovery.
recommendation: Remove network_mode: host and use explicit port mapping (e.g., ports: ['8000:8000']). Use Docker's internal DNS for service discovery between containers.

title: Multiple Hardcoded API URLs with Internal IPs
description: Hardcoded IP address 192.168.50.30 found in 9+ locations across frontend codebase including api.js, SetupWizard.js, DevicesPage.js, AlertsPage.js, and AgentsPage.js.
impact: Production deployments will fail if backend isn't at this exact IP. Internal network topology exposed in source code.
recommendation: Use environment variable: const API_BASE_URL = process.env.REACT_APP_API_URL || '/api'. Update all fetch calls to use centralized api client.

title: Credentials Stored in LocalStorage
description: Setup data (potentially including router credentials) is persisted in browser localStorage indefinitely via saveProgress function.
impact: Router credentials may persist in browser storage, accessible to any JavaScript running on the domain including XSS payloads.
recommendation: Remove localStorage persistence for credential data. Use sessionStorage if needed and clear immediately after setup completion. Never store credentials in browser storage.

title: Invalid Health Check Ports (Typos)
description: Health check uses 'http://localhost:808000/health' and 'http://localhost:808080/' which are invalid port numbers (max 65535).
impact: Health checks will always fail, potentially causing containers to be marked as unhealthy and restarted continuously.
recommendation: Fix to 'http://localhost:8000/health' and 'http://localhost:8080/'. Review all healthcheck configurations for typos. Consider removing backup file if not needed.

title: Static Salt in PBKDF2 Key Derivation
description: The salt 'vigil-static-salt-32bytes-long!' is hardcoded. Using a static salt defeats the purpose of key derivation and makes rainbow table attacks possible.
impact: If database is compromised, all passwords vulnerable to rainbow table attacks. Static salt provides no additional security over unsalted hashes.
recommendation: Generate a random salt for each encryption operation and store it alongside the encrypted data. Use at least 16 bytes of cryptographically secure random data.

title: Command Injection via Subprocess
description: Multiple uses of asyncio.create_subprocess_exec with user-controlled IP addresses passed directly to commands (nmblookup, snmpget, avahi-browse).
impact: While create_subprocess_exec is safer than shell=True, an attacker controlling IP could inject command arguments leading to arbitrary command execution.
recommendation: Validate IP addresses using ipaddress module before subprocess execution. Reject non-private IPs and invalid formats. Consider allowlisting allowed commands.

title: XML External Entity (XXE) Vulnerability
description: ET.fromstring() parses XML without security configuration, vulnerable to XXE attacks allowing file disclosure and SSRF.
impact: Attackers can read arbitrary files (file:///etc/passwd), perform SSRF attacks, or cause DoS via billion laughs attack.
recommendation: Replace with defusedxml.ElementTree or configure XMLParser with resolve_entities=False and forbid_external=True.

title: Containers Run as Root User
description: No USER directive specified in Dockerfiles, containers run as root. If application is compromised, attacker has root privileges.
impact: Privilege escalation if container is compromised. Container escape becomes host compromise immediately.
recommendation: Add 'RUN useradd -m -u 1000 appuser && chown -R appuser:appuser /app' and 'USER appuser' before CMD instruction.

title: SSH Host Key Verification Disabled
description: StrictHostKeyChecking=no and UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null completely disable SSH security for deployment connection.
impact: Makes deployment vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. An attacker could intercept the SSH connection to GX-10.
recommendation: Remove StrictHostKeyChecking=no. Use ssh-keyscan to populate known_hosts, or use the webfactory/ssh-agent action which handles this properly.

title: Hardcoded Absolute Database Paths
description: Database path '/home/erik-ross/projects/vigil-home/vigil.db' hardcoded in multiple files. Will fail on any other machine.
impact: Application will fail to start in any environment other than the developer's machine. Creates security risk if path traverses into user directories.
recommendation: Use environment variable DATABASE_PATH consistently across all files. Validate path is within allowed directories.

title: Static Salt in Vault Encryption
description: Static salt (b'vigil-credential-vault-v1') used for PBKDF2 key derivation enables rainbow table attacks.
impact: Precomputation attacks possible. Attackers can build rainbow tables for common passwords and crack credentials if database is compromised.
recommendation: Use random per-credential salt stored alongside encrypted data. Salt should be unique for each encryption operation.

title: JWT Token Transmitted via URL Query Parameter
description: JWT tokens can be passed via ?token=<JWT> query parameter for SSE streams. Tokens appear in server logs, Referer headers, browser history.
impact: Tokens exposed in access logs, browser history, and Referer headers. Increased risk of token theft and replay attacks.
recommendation: Use cookie-based authentication with HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict flags for SSE. Implement short-lived token exchange pattern.

title: Predictable Fallback Encryption Key
description: The fallback key 'fallback-key-32bytes-long!!!!!' is easily guessable. Used if VIGIL_KEY environment variable not set.
impact: If database is leaked, encrypted credentials can be decrypted by anyone knowing the predictable fallback key.
recommendation: Require VIGIL_KEY to be set at startup. Fail fast with clear error message if not configured. Never use hardcoded keys.

title: XSS Risk: Unsanitized Content Rendering
description: event.details, alert.description, alert.narrative rendered directly without sanitization. Potential XSS if backend data compromised.
impact: If attacker can inject malicious content into event/alert data, they can execute arbitrary JavaScript in user browsers.
recommendation: Install and use DOMPurify to sanitize HTML content before rendering. Or ensure content is treated as plain text only.

title: No CSRF Protection in API Client
description: No CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie configuration for state-changing operations. API calls lack CSRF protection.
impact: Attackers can trick authenticated users into performing unwanted actions (state-changing requests) via malicious websites.
recommendation: Add CSRF token header to all requests. Implement double-submit cookie pattern. Use SameSite=Strict cookies.

title: Auth Bypass Environment Variable
description: VIGIL_AUTH_DISABLED environment variable can completely disable authentication. If accidentally set in production, exposes entire API.
impact: Simple environment variable can completely bypass authentication. Entire API exposed without authentication if set.
recommendation: Add check to prevent AUTH_DISABLED=True when VIGIL_ENV=production. Log critical warning if attempted. Require explicit confirmation.

title: Hardcoded Database Password with Weak Fallback
description: POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-vigil_secure_2024} uses predictable default password if env var not set.
impact: If environment variable not set, database uses predictable default password, easily guessable by attackers.
recommendation: Remove the default value. Fail fast if password not provided via environment variable or Docker secret.
